text: Does the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV (BKdV) system decompose a localized Gaussian wave packet in v (amp=4, sigma=1.5, centered at x=-5) with u=0 initially into a train of solitons (>=2 well-separated peaks with amplitude >=0.8) by T=6.0, while keeping mass(v) drift < 8%?

method: Fourier pseudospectral (no dealiasing) + explicit RK4 over full RHS including v_xxx; periodic [-15,15], Nx=256
bank_use_rationale: Progressive-complexity discipline mandates the simplest baseline first; kb-kdv-IMEX-CN-spectral-pass merely confirms Fourier pseudospectral is the right discretization family for v_xxx (we use explicit RK4 in lieu of IMEX-CN to remain at baseline). No dealiasing yet -- we must observe whether amp=4 Gaussian triggers aliasing failure even before adding the 2/3 cutoff. The IC is kept as the reference Gaussian with u=0 (NOT the m=0 BKdV-S7 sech^2 adaptation); the task explicitly targets Gaussian decomposition and adapting to sech^2+u=v^2/2 would change the physical scenario.

diagnostics.warning_chain: overflow in v*v -> NaN in FFT -> NaN propagation

rationale: Baseline Fourier pseudospectral + RK4 with NO dealiasing fails by aliasing-driven overflow within ~30 steps. With amp=4 Gaussian (sup(v)=4, sup(v^2)=16), quadratic products v^2, uv inject energy outside the resolved band; these alias back, feed v_xxx-amplified high-k modes, and overflow before any physical evolution occurs. Failure mode is exactly the aliasing-amplification scenario kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation and BKdV-S1 warn against, motivating the single-component upgrade to 2/3 dealiasing for E2.

Single-component upgrade: add 2/3-rule dealiasing to all nonlinear products (per BKdV-S1 r1 which validated the same stack passes T=10 cleanly at amp=3); keep everything else identical (Nx=256, L=30, dt=2e-4, RK4, same IC).

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product (v^2, uv, vv_x, uu_x) + explicit RK4 over full RHS; periodic [-15,15], Nx=256
bank_use_rationale: Single-component upgrade vs E1: add 2/3-rule dealiasing only. BKdV-S1 round1 directly validated this exact stack (Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealias + RK4 dt=2e-4 Nx=256 L=30) reaching T=10 with mass conserved to 1e-12 at amp=1.5; round2 confirmed the same stack survives amp=3 with sup_v decaying as expected. kb-gardner-G2-IMEX-CN-dealiased-stableRadiation independently endorses 2/3 dealiasing for the Gardner-reduction sector. kb-kdv-spectral-solitonAmplitude-conservation endorses spectral methods for tracking soliton amplitude/phase in Gaussian decomposition tasks. BKdV-S7 round2 supplies the physical prediction that coupled BKdV fragments a smooth v-pulse into a multi-peak structure on O(1) time (n_peaks 1->8 from sech^2 IC), which is the target phenomenon for the Gaussian IC. We retain explicit RK4 rather than IMEX-CN because the post-dealias dispersion CFL ~4.94e-4 leaves dt=2e-4 with ~2.5x margin (per BKdV-S1 round1 analysis), so adding implicit treatment now would be a second simultaneous component change (violation).

rationale: Stack from BKdV-S1 r1/r2 (Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealias + RK4 at Nx=256, dt=2e-4) transferred cleanly from sech^2 ICs to the amp=4 Gaussian: reached T=6 with no blowup, mass_v conserved to machine zero (drift 0.0%), and v fragmented from a single Gaussian into 15 distinct peaks above 0.8, with multiple well-separated peaks (e.g. x=-9.7 v=3.79 and x=-3.5 v=4.85, ~6.2 apart). Phenomenon target (>=2 well-separated peaks of amp>=0.8, mass drift <8%) is clearly met. Physical mechanism matches BKdV-S7 r2's prediction: coupled -d_x(uv) destroys the m=0-style integrability and fragments the v-pulse on O(1) time. u grows to |u|_inf=20.24 (acceptable; not asked for in target).
Phenomenon target met with substantial margin (15 peaks vs >=2 required, mass drift 0% vs <8% budget). No further iteration needed; stop early to conserve budget.
